failure_surface: Execution failures due to improper code block formatting (XML-style <function_code> tags instead of markdown), combined with potential mismatch between specified answer position (column B only) and implemented output (columns B and C)
root_cause: The agent repeatedly wrapped Python code in <function_code> XML-style tags rather than standard markdown code blocks, causing SyntaxError on each initial attempt. Although the final run succeeded after using markdown formatting, the code wrote to both columns B and C (columns 2 and 3) when the answer position indicated only column B (B11:B17) was expected.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all executable code uses standard markdown triple-backtick code blocks without XML-style wrapper tags, and verify that write operations target exactly the columns/sheets specified in the task requirements rather than assuming additional output columns are needed.
evidence: Cell In[1], line 1 \n    <function_code>\n    ^SyntaxError: invalid syntax - appears 3 times in conversation_tail
Final successful output: Copied 7 rows to Output sheet - achieved only after switching to markdown code blocks
Answer position specified as Output'!B11:B17 but code writes to columns 2 and 3 (both B and C)

memory_items.title: Code Block Formatting Protocol
memory_items.lesson: When executing Python code in notebook environments, always wrap code in standard markdown triple-backtick blocks (```python ... ```) rather than XML-style tags like <function_code>.
memory_items.generalization: Execution environment compatibility requires adherence to the expected code block format; XML tags trigger syntax errors in Python interpreters.

memory_items.title: Target Range Verification
memory_items.lesson: Before implementing data write operations, confirm the exact target columns and ranges specified in the task requirements do not exceed what is requested.
memory_items.generalization: Write operations to unintended columns constitute silent failures even if code executes successfully; validate column indices against specification.

memory_items.title: Iteration-Driven Recovery
memory_items.lesson: When facing repeated syntax errors, immediately recognize the pattern and switch formatting strategy rather than repeating the same mistake.
memory_items.generalization: Consistent error patterns across multiple iterations indicate environmental/tooling incompatibility requiring immediate correction.

edits.content:
## Execution Hygiene
- **Code Block Formatting:** Always use standard markdown triple-backtick code blocks (```python ... ```) for executable code. XML-style tags (<function_code>, etc.) cause SyntaxError in Python interpreters.
- **Target Validation:** Verify output column/range specifications match task requirements exactly. Writing to unrequested columns counts as failure even if code runs successfully.
- **Error Pattern Recognition:** When identical errors repeat across multiple attempts, immediately diagnose environmental/tooling incompatibility and adjust the approach rather than retrying identically.
